title: Use of Tracer Dyes to Label Neural Projections to Lower Urinary Tract Organs
author: Janet R Keast
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.w2xfgfn
materials: Prepare tracer dye solutions: Fluorogold or Fast Blue (each 2% w/v in sterile water).
Shave and clean the ventral abdomen.

steps.title: Anesthetise animal (2
steps.instruction: Anesthetise animal (2.5% isoflurane in oxygen, or as required for maintenance)

steps.title: Apply eye lubricant and place animal on heated pad
steps.instruction: Apply eye lubricant and place animal on heated pad.

steps.title: Perform a midline incision in the...
steps.instruction: Perform a midline incision in the skin and then the muscle, then gently move organs to visualise the required injection site.

steps.title: Microinject sterile tracer solution at the...
steps.instruction: Microinject sterile tracer solution at the selected injection site using a Hamilton Neuros Syringe attached to a 33G needle. At each injection site, hold the needle in place for ~5 seconds after ejection of the dye, to enable the dye to spread to the underlying tissue. This also minimises leakage. Injections into the bladder body (dome) are made bilaterally on dorsal and ventral aspects; total volume ~ 5 µl. Single injections are made into the bladder trigone or dorsal aspect of the proximal urethra (maximum 0.3 µl per injection).

steps.title: Wash all injection sites with sterile saline
steps.instruction: Wash all injection sites with sterile saline.

steps.title: Close the muscle and skin using approved procedures
steps.instruction: Close the muscle and skin using approved procedures. Administer analgesics and monitor animal during postoperative period as per local approved procedure.

steps.title: To analyse tracer dye distribution in...
steps.instruction: To analyse tracer dye distribution in ganglia or the injection site, 5-14 days after surgery, fix animals by intra-cardiac perfusion, then remove tissues of interest for further study. For studies of the lower urinary tract innervation, these tissues would typically include dorsal root ganglia (DRG) from L1, L2, L6 and S1 spinal levels and the pelvic ganglia (synonym, major pelvic ganglia). It is also recommended that the lower urinary tract tissues are also removed for microscopic validation of the tracer injection site.
